title: The principle of topological interlocking in extraterrestrial construction
supportingExtract: Applications of a newly established principle of topological interlocking to different types of extraterrestrial construction are considered. This paper introduces the principle of topological interlocking and considers conceptually some applications in both planetary and space construction.

title: Geotechnical Engineering Considerations for Extraterrestrial Infrastructure Construction
supportingExtract: This paper has been prepared for inclusion in the ASCE Geo Institute Geotechnical Special Publication honoring our distinguished colleague, Clyde N. Baker, Jr. ... The purposes of this paper are to summarize current knowledge and understanding of the types and geotechnical properties of the soils on the Moon and Mars, and to identify some important considerations in geotechnical engineering for safe operations and facility design and construction in the unique and harsh environmental conditions encountered there.

title: Soils as extended composite phenotypes
supportingExtract: The notion of genetic signatures in soil morphology also has implications for the search for extraterrestrial life, and extends the notion of Earth as a set of tightly-coupled, densely interwoven systems.

title: Laboratory-Scale Distributed Pressure Measurements of Blade Interaction with JSC-1A Lunar Simulant
supportingExtract: Because of the requirement for high reliability and very low weight, extraterrestrial excavator blade design for in situ resource utilization (ISRU) must be more precise than for current terrestrial practice. ... This project measured the pressure distribution on a suite of laboratory-scale blades. ... The work concluded that a V-shaped blade with a tapered curve is the best con guration

title: Considering Construction of Conservative/Liberal Meaning: What an Extraterrestrial Might Discover About Branding Strategy in the C-SPAN Video Library
supportingExtract: In this study of what constructed meaning in media discourse reveals about recent changes in political semantics, the power of political branding strategy is demonstrated through a remarkable lesson in not missing the forest for the trees.

title: Microwave processing of lunar soil for supporting longer-term surface exploration on the Moon
supportingExtract: This article reviews our current understanding of the dielectric behaviour of lunar soil in the micro- wave spectrum... We also review the existing literature on the microwave processing of lunar soil and identify three key research areas where future efforts are needed

title: Effect of grain size distribution on stress-strain behavior of lunar soil simulants
supportingExtract: The goal of this study is to investigate how and to what extent the variations in the grain size distribution of different lunar soil simulants affect their shear strength and volume change behaviors, both of which are quite important for constitutive modeling and geotechnical design.

title: A microbial role in the construction of Mono Lake carbonate chimneys?
supportingExtract: Lacustrine carbonate chimneys are striking, metre-scale constructions. If these were bioinfluenced constructions, they could be priority targets in the search for early and extraterrestrial microbial life. Here, we use correlative microscopy to show that microbes were living around Pleistocene Mono Lake carbonate chimneys during their growth.

title: SPACE RESEARCH AND EXTRATERRESTRIAL CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRY
supportingExtract: In the present article, an overview of space research is presented together with the innovations awaited in space construction industry and the importance of feedback of these studies to construction industry in general.

title: The Multiplanetary Future of Plant Synthetic Biology
supportingExtract: In this article, we extend the existing body of work on the use of microbes [8–11] and argue that synthetic biology will provide the means for outpacing terrestrial evolution to take full advantage of plants beyond Earth.

title: Fabrication of Flexible Thermoelectric Energy Harvesting System
supportingExtract: This paper describes procedures incurred in the fabrication of thermoelectric energy harvester on flexible substrates. An electrically parallel structure is proposed, which features higher energy efficiency than the counterpart electrically series structure based on theoretical analyses. As proof of concept, flexible thermoelectric harvesters in electrically parallel and series structures are prepared with thick film printing procedures.

title: Soil Mechanics in Vacuum Chamber
supportingExtract: This study presents a new approach in soil mechanics that is particularly applicable to the dust vacuum chamber. One of the main issues in operating the dust vacuum chamber is soil disturbance problems during the evacuation... This study suggested a new operating guideline to minimize the soil disturbance in dust vacuum chamber.

title: Thermal and Mechanical Properties of Cementitious Composites for Additive Construction of Energy-Saving Habitats
supportingExtract: Because of its advantages in reducing both structural weight and thermal conductivity, it has the potential to be used in the construction of terrestrial and extraterrestrial habits. Furthermore, since it has relatively small and spherical shaped aggregates, LWCC is also suitable for extrusion based 3-D printing. In this research, a comprehensive and thorough study was carried out to investigate the effects of different lightweight fillers on both the mechanical and thermal properties of LWCC.

title: Multiscale Modeling and Testing of Protein-Bound Regolith and Soils
supportingExtract: This paper proposes a multi-scale framework to model the composite’s fracture properties in order to design a durable material that can resist extreme environments... The final goal of this project is to have a robust computational simulation that can enable the prediction of the mechanical properties of the protein-bound regolith material to be used to design future structures on the Moon, Mars, or other celestial bodies.

title: Materials and design concepts for space-resilient structures
supportingExtract: This paper presents a state-of-the-art literature review on recent developments of “space-native” construction materials, and highlights evolutionary design concepts for “space-resilient” structures (i.e., colonies and habitats).

title: Multiobjective Optimization for Structural Design of Lunar Habitats
supportingExtract: Earth and Space 2018 169 © ASCE Multiobjective Optimization for Structural Design of Lunar Habitats... While the cases presented here are limited to lunar surface systems, both the general architectures and the methodology for analysis and design are applicable to future Mars settlements.

title: Arching Effect of Planetary Regolith Simulant under Extraterrestrial Gravities
supportingExtract: This paper studies the influence of extraterrestrial gravities on the arching effect surrounding deeply buried structures in planetary regolith using trapdoor experiments. Off-earth regoliths are physically simulated using a magnetically sensitive planetary regolith simulant, whereas the gravities are simulated by applying a uniform magnetic body force.

title: Extraterrestrial construction materials
supportingExtract: This review explores the suitability of construction materials derived from lunar and Martian regolith along with concrete derivatives, space-native metals and composites, as well as advanced and non-traditional materials for interplanetary construction. This review also identifies processing techniques suitable to produce non-terrestrial construction materials in the alien environment of space (i.e. vacuum, low gravity, etc.)

title: Solar 3D printing of lunar regolith
publisher: Elsevier Ltd on behalf of IAA
supportingExtract: In this work, the authors combined concentrated sunlight with a 3D printing process to create the first 3D printer capable of creating three-dimensional objects made of lunar regolith simulant sintered layer by layer by concentrated solar light.

title: Space-native construction materials for earth-independent and sustainable infrastructure
supportingExtract: This review covers feasibility of exploiting in-situ lunar and Martian re- sources as well as harvesting of elements and compounds, from near Earth objects (NEOs), to produce extra- terrestrial materials suitable for construction of space-based infrastructure. In es- sence, this paper reviews past and recent advancements in construction-based materials that could be used in the design and development of space human bases and highlights design consideration for sustainable human set- tlements.

title: Some Fuzzy Measures Constructing Paradigm and Methods From Given Measure Spaces and Evaluation Information
supportingExtract: In this article, we propose a constructing paradigm for generating fuzzy measures in some certain decision situations, such as in risk evaluation and decision making. The constructing paradigm involves four constructing factors: source measure space, construction function, construction choice, and object/constructed measure space.

title: A review towards the design of extraterrestrial structures: From regolith to human outposts
publisher: Elsevier Ltd on behalf of IAA
supportingExtract: The scope of this paper is to present a meticulous selection of the most significant publications within the scientific areas related to: (a) geotechnical engineering aspects... (b) ground motions generated by different types of Moonquakes and meteoroid impacts; (c) the different concepts and types of extraterrestrial (ET) structures

title: Topological design of a new family of legged mobile landers based on Truss-Mechanism Transformation method
supportingExtract: This paper aims to address a novel synthesis method based on Truss-Mechanism Transformation (called as TMT method) for LMLs with the capabilities of trusses and mechanisms during different phases. ... To meet the needs of the “Returning” and “Base construction” missions of the extraterrestrial body’s exploration task, it is necessary to enable the legged stationary lander (LSL) to walk

title: Ultrasonic tool for the realization of combined action during the drilling of extraterrestrial objects
supportingExtract: The article is devoted to the problem of the efficiency increase of ultrasonic drilling of hard materials for the studies of the soils of the planets, asteroids and other space objects. It is proposed the solution of the problem by the help of developed device, which is able to realize simultaneously with ultrasound the action by low-frequency macro-oscillations and rotational motion of the leading edge of the tool.

title: A Framework for Extraterrestrial Environmental Assessment
supportingExtract: In addition to advocating the need for an appropriate assessment and review process, this article proposes a simple framework as a starting point for discussion.

title: Mechanical behavior of the metal parts welded with extraterrestrial regolith simulant by the solar concentrator in ISRU & ISRF application
supportingExtract: This paper proposes a method for welding metal together with extraterrestrial regolith simulant by solar concentrator at ambient conditions. Herein the welding procedure is introduced, and the mechanical properties of weldments was tested by the tensile instrument. Finally, the microstructure of fusion zone of weldments is characterized.
